stimulus.content: After the Constitution was written in 1787, Americans debated whether to approve it. Federalists supported the new plan because it created a stronger national government. Anti-Federalists worried it gave too much power to the central government and did not protect individual rights.

stem: What was the main concern of the Anti-Federalists?

choices.text: The national government would be too weak
The central government would have too much power
The states would have no role in government
The president would serve for life in office

answer_explanation: The passage states Anti-Federalists worried the Constitution gave too much power to the central government and did not protect individual rights. The other choices are not stated in the passage.
